2603.24014.title: Language-Grounded Multi-Agent Planning for Personalized and Fair Participatory Urban Sensing
2603.24014.paper_summary: Participatory urban sensing leverages human mobility for large-scale urban data collection, yet existing methods typically rely on centralized optimization and assume homogeneous participants, resulting in rigid assignments that overlook personal preferences and heterogeneous urban contexts. We propose MAPUS, an LLM-based multi-agent framework for personalized and fair participatory urban sensing. In our framework, participants are modeled as autonomous agents with individual profiles and schedules, while a coordinator agent performs fairness-aware selection and refines sensing routes through language-based negotiation. Experiments on real-world datasets show that MAPUS achieves competitive sensing coverage while substantially improving participant satisfaction and fairness, promoting more human-centric and sustainable urban sensing systems.
2603.24014.paper_summary_zh: 參與式城市感知利用人類移動性進行大規模城市數據收集，但現有方法通常依賴集中優化並假設參與者是同質的，導致僵化的分配忽略了個人偏好和異質的城市背景。我們提出了MAPUS，一個基於LLM的多代理框架，用於個性化和公平的參與式城市感知。在我們的框架中，參與者被建模為具有個人檔案和時間表的自主代理，而協調代理則進行公平意識的選擇並通過基於語言的協商來優化感知路徑。在真實世界數據集上的實驗表明，MAPUS實現了具有競爭力的感知覆蓋，同時顯著提高了參與者的滿意度和公平性，促進了更以人為本和可持續的城市感知系統。
2603.24014.author: Xusen Guo et.al.
2603.24014.authors: Xusen Guo, Mingxing Peng, Hongliang Lu, Hai Yang, Jun Ma, Yuxuan Liang
